evals.expected_output: A Conventional Commits message with type 'feat', scope 'auth', imperative description, and a body bullet explaining WHY (users were silently logged out).
assertions: Description is imperative, lowercase, no trailing period
Body contains at least one '- ' bullet point
No '!' in header (not a breaking change)
All lines are 80 characters or fewer

evals.prompt:
Generate a commit message for this diff:

```diff
diff --git a/api/users.py b/api/users.py
index 1234567..abcdefg 100644
--- a/api/users.py
+++ b/api/users.py
@@ -45,7 +45,7 @@ class UserSchema(Schema):
     class Meta:
         fields = ('id', 'email', 'created_at', 'updated_at')
 
-    name = fields.Str(required=True)
+    first_name = fields.Str(required=True)
+    last_name = fields.Str(required=True)
 
-    def get_full_name(self):
-        return self.name
+    def get_full_name(self):
+        return f'{self.first_name} {self.last_name}'
```
evals.expected_output: A BREAKING CHANGE commit message — 'name' renamed to 'first_name'/'last_name' breaks the API. Should use '!' after type/scope and body explaining the migration path.
assertions: Header contains '!' before ':' signaling a breaking change
Body mentions migration path (first_name and/or last_name)
Body uses '- ' bullet points, not prose paragraphs
All lines are 80 characters or fewer

evals.prompt:
Generate a commit message for this diff:

```diff
diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index aaa1111..bbb2222 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -1,4 +1,4 @@
-# My Proejct
+# My Project
 
 ## Installation
```
evals.expected_output: A minimal 'docs' commit with no body (typo fix is trivial). Header only: 'docs: fix typo in README title'.
assertions: No body present (trivial change; body should be omitted)
Description mentions typo or README or title
All lines are 80 characters or fewer

evals.expected_output: A 'feat' or 'refactor' commit with scope 'db' or 'query', body explaining the motivation (filtering was hardcoded, now parameterized). Lines ≤ 80 chars, bullets not prose.
assertions: Type is 'feat' or 'refactor' with a scope (db or query)
Body explains motivation: filtering was hardcoded, now supports dynamic conditions
Body uses '- ' bullet points, not prose paragraphs
All lines are 80 characters or fewer
